title: Research phase dedup is O(N*M) — per-finding search_findings loads all embeddings and re-embeds each query
description:
In runner.py _phase_research() (lines ~377-386), each finding from each agent is dedup-checked individually:

    for finding in result.findings:
        existing = memory.search_findings(self.db, f'{title}. {summary}', limit=1, days=10)

Each call to search_findings (findings.py:532-599):
1. Calls embed_text(query) — one model inference per finding
2. Loads ALL rows from findings + findings_embeddings tables for the last 10 days
3. Deserializes ALL embeddings and computes cosine similarity against each

With 13 agents × ~20 findings = ~260 findings, and a 10-day DB of ~2600 existing findings, this is:
- 260 separate embedding model inferences (vs 1 batch call with embed_texts)
- 260 × full table scan of ~2600 rows = loading ~676K rows total
- 260 × 2600 = 676K similarity computations

The embed_texts() batch function exists in memory/embeddings.py but is unused here.

Additionally, store_finding() (findings.py:501-529) calls db.commit() after each individual insert, forcing ~260 separate fsyncs.

Fix: batch-embed all new findings, load existing embeddings once, vectorize dedup with numpy, and wrap all inserts in a single transaction.
requirements: Add a batch_dedup_findings(db, findings_list, days=10, threshold=0.90) function to memory/findings.py that batch-embeds all candidates, loads existing embeddings once, and returns the deduplicated list
Use embed_texts() for batch embedding instead of per-finding embed_text()
Use numpy matrix multiplication for vectorized similarity instead of per-row dot product
Wrap the entire finding storage loop in _phase_research in a single db transaction (one commit at the end)
Update store_finding() to accept an optional pre_computed_embedding parameter to avoid re-embedding during storage
done_criteria: Research phase dedup+storage time reduced by at least 50% on a typical run (260 findings, 2600 existing)
embed_text is called once per batch (not per finding) during dedup
DB rows are loaded once per dedup batch, not once per finding
Only one db.commit() call for the entire finding storage loop
All existing tests pass (python3 -m pytest tests/ -x -q)
expected_impact: Reduces research phase dedup+storage from ~260 embedding calls + 260 full table scans + 260 commits to 1 batch embed + 1 table scan + 1 commit. Estimated 3-5x speedup on the finding storage portion of the research phase.
context: Distinct from 2026-04-01-R010 (embedding cache across runs). This ticket addresses the O(N*M) algorithmic issue within a single run — even with a perfect cache, calling search_findings 260 times still loads all DB rows 260 times. The cache ticket (R010) and this ticket are complementary: the cache reduces per-embedding cost, this ticket reduces the number of operations.
